body_txt: A character who really, really likes their sweets . Everything they eat could be classed as a dessert and if they drink tea or coffee it's got so much sugar in it that the smell alone could kill a diabetic. Sometimes identified as fuel for a hyperactive character. Another common version is for the Genius (e.g. the Gadgeteer Genius , Genius Ditz or Teen Genius ) to require lots of sweets — they burn the sugars by thinking extra hard. Something of a Truth in Television : the brain uses glucose as its primary source of energy and not getting enough is bad. Real Life examples of the Sweet Tooth heed warning, however, because having too much sugar is also bad — not to mention the risk of cavities . With adult characters, the same effect can be achieved with much lower doses—after all, most kids are nuts about the stuff to begin with. One frequent shorthand is to show someone adding huge amounts of sugar to tea or coffee (especially the Klatchian Coffee , sometimes to the extent of an Overly Long Gag ), with a bystander looking on in horror. Another reason for an adult being obsessed with sugar can be to emphasise their immaturity (or more positively, "being in touch with their inner child"). May have some of the tendencies of the Big Eater . If they particularly enjoy lollipops as their Trademark Favorite Food , you may have an Oral Fixation on your hands. In Edutainment shows, this may lead to An Aesop about tooth decay, or instead, A Weighty Aesop . Compare and contrast with Real Men Hate Sugar . Super-Trope of Mascots Love Sugar (for critters), Girls Love Chocolate (for female characters), Genius Sweet Tooth (for smart characters), and Sinister Sweet Tooth (for villains). Can suffer from a Lethally Bad Diet if they experience negative health effects from so many sweets. Not to be confused with the comic , show and The Monster Clown from Twisted Metal of the same name.
